content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 25, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 25, 2026, focused on a major transition of the ElizaOS ecosystem to Gemma 4 model defaults, significant performance optimizations for local inference and boot times, and extensive hardening of the voice and vision pipelines. The team also completed a comprehensive audit of LifeOps benchmarks and implemented rigorous type-safety ratchets across the codebase to improve long-term stability.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

*   **Gemma 4 Ecosystem Transition:** Completed a massive migration of training, RL, local inference, and cloud defaults from Qwen to Gemma 4 (E2B/E4B/12B/31B). This included updating registry entries, trajectory targets, and training scripts to ensure full compatibility with the new Gemma-based Eliza-1 release tiers. ([#9742](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9742), [#9740](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9740), [#9734](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9734), [#9707](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9707))
*   **Voice Pipeline & AEC Hardening:** Implemented advanced acoustic echo cancellation (AEC) primitives, including playback-to-mic delay calibration and echo-reference alignment buffers. These updates, combined with the successful activation of the Kokoro TTS engine, significantly improve live half-duplex voice interactions. ([#9633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9633), [#9596](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9596), [#9586](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9586), [#9575](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9575))
*   **Performance & Boot Optimization:** Optimized agent startup readiness by reducing first-paint times and implementing telemetry-driven boot phase breakdowns. Hardened computer-use and vision hosts by replacing cold-spawn processes with persistent, warm hosts to eliminate significant latency on Windows. ([#9595](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9595), [#9645](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9645), [#9651](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9651), [#9727](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9727))
*   **Type-Safety & Build Infrastructure:** Advanced the type-safety ratchet by eliminating unsafe `as unknown as` casts and enforcing real type-checking across multiple plugins. Refactored Turbo build tasks to scope inputs, significantly improving build cache efficiency. ([#9627](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9627), [#9630](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9630), [#9708](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9708))

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#9752](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9752) feat(agent): filter chat doc retrieval by the requester's access context
*   [#9749](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9749) fix(ui): drop blue from memory-type badge + a couple cloud surfaces
*   [#9747](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9747) ci: fail Hetzner E2E on invalid cloud auth
*   [#9745](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9745) test(cloud): lock the ai-pricing provider-outage resilience contract
*   [#9744](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9744) test(cloud): fix red failure-writeback test
*   [#9743](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9743) fix(ui): use the story MockTranslationProvider in Storybook
*   [#9725](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9725) feat(app): harden desktop shell and view apps

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   *No specific issues were marked as closed in the provided activity log.*

## NEW ISSUES
